title: Great Ashfield — 385th Bomb Group Memorials
crew_summary: 400+ USAAF personnel killed in operations 1943–1945; 129 B-17s lost over 296 combat missions
memorial_type: Multiple — airfield gate plaque, church altar and reredos, stained glass window

multiple_memorials.inscription: IN MEMORIAM / DEDICATED / TO THE EVERLASTING MEMORY / OF THE OFFICERS AND MEN / OF THE 385TH BOMBARDMENT GROUP / 8TH AIR FORCE / US ARMY / WHO GAVE THEIR LIVES IN HEROIC SACRIFICE AND UNSELFISH DEVOTION TO DUTY / WORLD WAR II / MCMXLV

memorial_access_notes: All Saints Church is open to visitors. The airfield gate plaque is at the church perimeter. The former airfield is now largely agricultural land.

external_narratives.summary: The 385th BGA maintains extensive records of the group's operations from Great Ashfield (USAAF Station 155), Suffolk. The group flew 296 combat missions between June 1943 and May 1945, losing 129 B-17s and 400+ men. Notable operations include targets across Germany, France, and occupied Europe. Their final sorties were humanitarian food drops over Holland in Operation Chowhound, May 1945.
external_narratives.key_details: 296 missions; 129 aircraft lost; 400+ killed; Glenn Miller performed at the base on reaching 200 missions; last missions were food drops over Holland (Operation Chowhound)

external_narratives.summary: Local history documentation of the Great Ashfield airfield and its connection with the 385th Bomb Group. The airfield was sold off in 1955 after the war. Some deteriorated wartime buildings remain on what is now largely agricultural land.
external_narratives.key_details: Airfield sold 1955; some wartime structures remain

local_contacts.role: Maintains the altar/reredos and stained glass window memorials inside All Saints Church

US non-profit monitoring and documenting American war memorials in Europe

decision_log.scope_note: Grouped entry covering all three Great Ashfield memorials (1944 gate plaque, 1945 church altar, 1991 stained glass window). Primary location pin: All Saints Church. Framed around the memorial and the men who flew from this base — losses occurred over occupied Europe but the memorial is in the UK.
